title: Dialect Portability, NoSQL Tradeoffs, Distribution, and Production Defense · SQL Query Room
summary: A team must move a browser prototype toward PostgreSQL, justify any non-relational component, and defend the complete data system. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.
Choose relational, document, key-value, graph, or mixed models from access, integrity, evolution, and consistency needs.
Explain replication, partitioning, distributed transactions, consistency choices, and failure boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dialect Portability
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 workshop artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 6; justify any non-relational component.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dialect Portability.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate standard relational intent from sqlite and postgresql syntax, typing, null, date, and feature behavior. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dialect Portability, keep Dialect Portability connected to executable relational evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Capstone Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-capstone-defense-evmb2b" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; dialect, storage-model, distribution, or production evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Capstone Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Capstone Defense into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Capstone Defense, keep Capstone Defense connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-capstone-defense-evmb2b
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS backlog
FROM tickets WHERE status <> 'resolved'
GROUP BY team_id ORDER BY team_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
